text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions are spiking with the Iran/Strait of Hormuz situation, causing oil surges and broad market uncertainty. This directly impacts your tech/chip names, which are sensitive to global stability and supply chains. Positive news from TSMC offers a counter-narrative for the chip sector.

Your holdings: SOXL is down -24.46% and flagged "SELL," needing immediate attention due to its leveraged decay. SPAL, another leveraged ETF, is new and already down -27.71%, amplifying risk on margin. CRWD is up 4.06% and looks solid.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the escalating Middle East conflict and TSMC's critical earnings report on Thursday.

Bottom line: Tighten stops on leveraged positions while navigating geopolitical risks and watching for chip sector resilience.
plays: SOXL: Down -24.46% and "SELL." Consider trimming this leveraged ETF to manage decay and amplified risk from market volatility.
SPAL: New and down -27.71%. Re-evaluate its stop at 21.38 given its volatility and significant loss on margin.
CRWD: Up 4.06% and an "ACCUMULATE" scanner signal (entry 186.84-186.65, stop 185.53). Consider adding if it aligns with your strategy.
